Items.Key: format string vulnerability in `format_string`|format string|cwe-134|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...);|attacker-controlled format strings can lead to information disclosure (stack/heap reads) or arbitrary code execution via %n writes.|passing user-supplied strings as the `fmt` parameter.|replace with `snprintf(buffer, buf_size, "%s", fmt);` or implement a safe formatting routine that validates format specifiers.->format string vulnerability in `format_string`|format string|cwe-134|high|0,9|enhanced_calc.cpp|218|223|string_utils::format_string|snprintf(buffer, buf_size, fmt, args...);|attacker-controlled format strings can lead to information disclosure (stack/heap reads) or arbitrary code execution via %n writes.|passing user-supplied strings as the `fmt` parameter.|replace with `snprintf(buffer, buf_size, "%s", fmt);` or implement a safe formatting routine that validates format specifiers.

truthAudit.Summary: Audit of Run 2 reveals 7 partially or fully identified vulnerabilities, 13 missed, and 1 unsupported finding. Run 2 consistently downgraded severities for critical issues and misclassified a heap overflow as a stack overflow. No new discoveries were credited to Run 2.

Items.Notes: Run 2 identified the format string vulnerability in log_debug_message but missed the log_format configuration anchor and downgraded severity to High instead of Critical.
Run 2 did not identify the integer overflow in the factorial computation loop.
Run 2 correctly identified the use-after-free during pool cleanup but missed the Critical severity and the sleep_for anchor in the lambda.

Items.PreviousOutputQuote:
std::string command = validator_cmd + " " + config_file;
            int result = system(command.c_str());
Items.Notes: Run 2 identified command injection via validate_config but missed the Critical severity and admin validate trigger context.
Run 2 identified the strcpy overflow in input processing but focused on the >= condition rather than the dest[src_len] out-of-bounds write pattern.
Run 2 exactly matched the race condition in the thread-safe counter, including the volatile long long and lock guard placement.
Run 2 perfectly captured the logic bomb/authentication bypass mechanism.
Run 2 did not identify path traversal in configuration loading.
Run 2 identified the strcpy overflow but incorrectly classified it as a stack buffer overflow (it uses heap allocation via g_memory_pool) and missed the Critical severity.
Run 2 identified the hardcoded secret but downgraded severity to Medium instead of High.
Run 2 did not identify the weak PRNG for session tokens.
Run 2 did not identify the thread-unsafe timestamp cache.
Run 2 did not identify insecure temporary file handling.
Run 2 did not identify persistent authentication state.
Run 2 did not identify unhandled exceptions in variable parsing.
Run 2 did not identify integer underflow in memory-pool block split.
Run 2 did not identify unbounded recursion in the parser.
Run 2 did not identify data race in the result cache.
Run 2 did not identify TOCTOU in config loading.
Run 2 did not identify integer division by zero in command routing.
